prompt:
The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping to two lines when the viewport gets
squeezed. This should never be collapsed or moved to the next line. It
should never compromise; this is a principle. Anything that's logo or
navigation, such a core identity line, should never ever be returned to
two lines when it's squeezed. The personal identity blurb, which is the
'Designer, AI systems experience...', that line CAN be squeezed into two
lines — that's fine. Fix the nav and codify the priority rule.
response:
## Diagnosis The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping because the flex container is allowing the logo text to shrink when sibling elements compete for space. The logo element lacks `whitespace-nowrap` and `flex-shrink-0`, so the browser's layout engine treats it as a shrinkable flex child — it will break before other elements do because it has no explicit protection. **Root cause: AP-072** — *Identity-critical text treated as an ordinary flex child.* No shrink guard or whitespace constraint is applied, so the logo participates in flex compression and wraps. --- ## Fix ### 1. The Nav component — protect the logo ```tsx // components/Nav/Nav.tsx (or wherever your nav is rendered) // BEFORE — logo has no shrink protection Yilan Gao // AFTER — logo is a rigid, never-wrap anchor Yilan Gao ``` The identity blurb (if it sits in the same flex row) gets the *opposite* treatment — it is allowed to wrap: ```tsx // The personal blurb — CAN wrap, that is intentional and acceptable

Designer, AI systems experience...

``` `min-w-0` is critical here: without it a flex child cannot shrink below its intrinsic content width, which would push the logo out instead. --- ### 2. Codify the rule in the design-system token file Create (or extend) a constraints file so this is never cargo-culted again: ```ts // lib/design-system/layout-constraints.ts /** * ELAN LAYOUT CONSTRAINTS * ======================= * These Tailwind class sets encode non-negotiable layout rules. * Import and apply them; do not inline equivalent ad-hoc classes. */ /** * AP-072 — Identity Rigid * * Applied to: wordmarks, logotypes, nav brand anchors. * Rule: An identity-critical text node MUST NEVER wrap or compress. * It sets its own floor; all siblings must yield. * * ✅ Use on: site name, brand mark, primary nav logo * ❌ Never use on: body copy, descriptors, taglines, blurbs */ export const identityRigid = [ "whitespace-nowrap", // no line breaks under any viewport "flex-shrink-0", // refuses to give up width in a flex row "flex-none", // excluded from flex grow/shrink calculations entirely "select-none", // wordmarks are not selectable text ] as const; /** * AP-072 inverse — Descriptor Yielding * * Applied to: taglines, blurbs, sub-identity copy. * Rule: These elements absorb all viewport pressure first. * They may wrap, truncate, or collapse — that is acceptable. * * ✅ Use on: "Designer, AI systems experience...", subtitles, captions * ❌ Never use on: logo, nav brand, primary CTA label */ export const descriptorYielding = [ "min-w-0", // allows this flex child to shrink below content width "break-words", // wraps gracefully rather than overflowing "flex-shrink", // explicitly participates in compression ] as const; ``` --- ### 3. Apply the token in the Nav ```tsx // components/Nav/Nav.tsx import { identityRigid, descriptorYielding } from "@/lib/design-system/layout-constraints"; import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; export function Nav() { return (
{/* IDENTITY — rigid, never wraps, never yields — AP-072 */} Yilan Gao {/* DESCRIPTOR — absorbs pressure, may wrap — AP-072 inverse */}

Designer, AI systems experience...

{/* nav links — push to the right, also protected from wrapping */}
    {/* ... */}
); } ``` --- ### 4. Add a Storybook / visual regression note (if applicable) ```ts // components/Nav/Nav.stories.tsx export const NarrowViewport: Story = { parameters: { // This is the critical test case for AP-072. // At 320px the logo MUST still be on one line. // The blurb may wrap — that is correct behavior. viewport: { defaultViewport: "mobile1" }, // 320×568 }, }; ``` --- ## Principle > **AP-072 — Identity Rigidity** > > A wordmark or brand name is not text — it is a spatial identity claim. It does not participate in flex compression, does not wrap, and does not negotiate with viewport pressure. Every pixel of layout budget is taken from descriptor copy, spacing, or secondary elements before the identity node is touched. Implement this with `flex-none whitespace-nowrap` at minimum; codify it as a named token so intent survives refactors.
